Package org.eventb.core.seqprover.eventbExtensions

Class AutoTactics.AbstractPredOnceTac

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.core.seqprover.eventbExtensions.AutoTactics.AbstractPredOnceTac
All Implemented Interfaces:
ITactic
Direct Known Subclasses:
AutoTactics.EqvRewritesAutoTac, AutoTactics.RiOnceTac, AutoTactics.RmOnceTac
Enclosing class:
AutoTactics
private abstract static class AutoTactics.AbstractPredOnceTac extends Object implements ITactic
Abstract implementation of an automatic tactic to apply once on a given collections of predicates. This class is intended to be used to wrap existing manual tactics.
Since:
2.1

  • Constructor Details

    • AbstractPredOnceTac

  • Method Details

    • apply

      public Object apply(IProofTreeNode ptNode, IProofMonitor pm)
      Description copied from interface: ITactic
      Applies this tactic to a proof tree at a specific node.

      The convention is that a tactic applied at a particular node is allowed to modify only the subtree rooted at that node, but this is not checked.

      Specified by:
      apply in interface ITactic
      Parameters:
      ptNode - The proof tree node at which this tactic should be applied
      pm - The proof monitor to monitor the progress of the tactic
      Returns:
      null iff the application was successfull.

      Note : The current convention for the return type is that in case the tactic was not successful, the proof tree is not modified, and the reason for tactic failure can be read from the toString() method from the object returned. In the near future the return type for the tactic will be refined to return more information useful for composing tactics. Implementors should therefore not rely on the return type at this time.

      See Also:
      • IProofMonitor

    • getPredicates

      protected abstract Iterable<Predicate> getPredicates(IProofTreeNode ptNode)
      Returns the predicates on which the tactic should apply on.
      Parameters:
      ptNode - the current proof tree node
      Returns:
      the predicates on which the tactic should apply on.

    • getPositions

      protected abstract List<IPosition> getPositions(Predicate predicate)
      Returns a list of positions at which the tactic can apply.
      Parameters:
      predicate - the predicate on which the positions are calculated
      Returns:
      the positions where the current tactic can apply on the given predicate

    • getTactic

      protected abstract ITactic getTactic(Predicate pred, IPosition pos)
      Returns the tactic to be applied on a given predicate at a given position.
      Parameters:
      pred - the predicate on which the tactic shall apply
      pos - the position where the tactic can apply
      Returns:
      the tactic to apply on the given predicate at the given position
